perturbation.original_text: MPL shall use reasonable commercial efforts to minimize the disruption of service on the Pipeline and any portion thereof. MPL shall promptly inform Shipper of any anticipated partial or complete disruption of service on the Pipeline, including relevant information about the nature, extent, cause and expected duration of the disruption and the actions MPL is taking to resume full operations, provided that MPL shall not have any liability for any failure to notify, or delay in notifying, Shipper of any such matters except to the extent Shipper has been materially prejudiced or damaged by such failure or delay.
perturbation.changed_text: MPL will endeavor, as it sees fit, to address service disruptions on the Pipeline. MPL will inform Shipper of disruptions as it deems appropriate, and will generally keep shipper abreast of material changes that impact product flow, but shall have no liability if notification isn't feasible.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text replaces "shall use reasonable commercial efforts" with "will endeavor, as it sees fit," making the obligation to minimize disruptions discretionary. It also replaces 'shall promptly inform' with 'will inform...as it deems appropriate' further weakening the notification obligation. This creates ambiguity around MPL's responsibility and liability for service disruptions.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification contradicts 49 CFR § 195.402, which mandates a comprehensive pipeline integrity program, including specific measures for identifying and addressing threats to pipeline integrity and incident notification. The ambiguous language weakens the requirement for proactive measures to minimize and promptly address disruptions, potentially leading to non-compliance with the mandated integrity program requirements. The operator must take specific and proactive measures; not just make efforts when it sees fit.

perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the obligation to minimize and report pipeline disruptions, creating ambiguity about MPL's responsibilities. This contradicts the requirement for a comprehensive pipeline integrity program under 49 CFR § 195.402, as explained by the LLM. The original text used "shall use reasonable commercial efforts" and "shall promptly inform," while the changed text uses "will endeavor, as it sees fit" and "will inform...as it deems appropriate," making the actions discretionary. This shift from mandatory to discretionary actions potentially leads to non-compliance with the mandated integrity program requirements.

perturbation.original_text: MPL shall maintain and repair all portions of the Pipeline in accordance with pipeline industry standards and in a manner which allows the Pipeline to be capable, subject to Force Majeure or temporary shutdown for pipeline testing and maintenance, of shipping, storing and delivering volumes of Product.
perturbation.changed_text: MPL intends to maintain and repair the Pipeline as it deems necessary, understanding that operations might be impacted due to unforeseen circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing "shall maintain and repair all portions of the Pipeline in accordance with pipeline industry standards" with "intends to maintain and repair the Pipeline as it deems necessary" introduces ambiguity, making maintenance subject to MPL's discretion rather than adhering to objective industry standards.  Adding "understanding that operations might be impacted due to unforeseen circumstances" could be interpreted as justifying a lower standard of maintenance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 49 CFR § 195.401 - General requirements for operation and maintenance
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification conflicts with 49 CFR § 195.401, which requires pipeline operators to maintain their pipelines in a condition that ensures safety and compliance with regulations. The ambiguous 'as it deems necessary' language weakens the commitment to objective industry standards, potentially leading to inadequate maintenance and non-compliance.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 49 CFR § 195.401 - General requirements. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 49 CFR § 195.401 - General requirements. CFR State Regulations prev | next § 195.401 General requirements. (a) No operator may operate or maintain its pipeline systems at a level of safety lower than that required by this subpart and the procedures it is required to establish under § 195.402(a) of this subpart. (b) An operator must make repairs on its pipeline system according to the following requirements: (1) Non Integrity management repairs. Whenever an operator discovers any condition that could adversely affect the safe operation of its pipeline system, it must correct the condition within a reasonable time. However, if the condition is of such a nature that it presents an immediate hazard to persons or property, the operator may not operate the affected part of the system until it has corrected the unsafe condition. (2) Integrity management repairs. When an operator discovers a condition on a pipeline covered under § 195.452 , the operator must correct the condition as prescribed in § 195.452(h) . (3) Prioritizing repairs. An operator must consider the risk to people, property, and the environment in prioritizing the correction of any conditions referenced in paragraphs (b)(1) and (2) of this section. (c) Except as provided in § 195.5 , no operator may operate any part of any of the following pipelines unless it was designed and constructed as required by this part: (1) An interstate pipeline , other than a low-stress pipeline , on which construction was begun after March 31, 1970, that transports hazardous liquid . (2) An interstate offshore gathering line , other than a low-stress pipeline , on which construction was begun after July 31, 1977, that transports hazardous liquid . (3) An intrastate pipeline , other than a low-stress pipeline , on which construction was begun after October 20, 1985
perturbation.justification: The original contract language mandates specific maintenance ("shall maintain and repair all portions of the Pipeline in accordance with pipeline industry standards"). The changed text replaces this with discretionary language ("intends to maintain and repair the Pipeline as it deems necessary"), weakening the obligation. This contradicts the general requirement in 49 CFR § 195.401(a) which states, "No operator may operate or maintain its pipeline systems at a level of safety lower than that required by this subpart and the procedures it is required to establish under § 195.402(a) of this subpart."

perturbation.original_text: Shipper shall provide to MPL, at any time: (i) upon MPL's request, information ("Financial Information") that will allow MPL to assess (or reassess) and establish creditworthiness and Shipper's capacity to perform any financial obligations that could arise from the transportation of Shipper's Crude Petroleum on the Pipeline; and (ii) upon MPL's good faith determination (which shall be no less than industry standards) of non-creditworthiness, MPL may request, financial assurance in respect of transportation or other services ("Financial Assurances").
perturbation.changed_text: Shipper will endeavor to provide information to MPL when reasonably able, allowing MPL to assess creditworthiness; however, MPL's determination of non-creditworthiness shall be at its sole discretion, and financial assurances may be requested without specific justification.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “shall provide to MPL, at any time: (i) upon MPL's request, information...” to “Shipper will endeavor to provide information to MPL when reasonably able...” introduces an ambiguity regarding the mandatory nature of the information. Additionally, by altering 'upon MPL's good faith determination (which shall be no less than industry standards)' to 'MPL's determination of non-creditworthiness shall be at its sole discretion, and financial assurances may be requested without specific justification' provides MPL broad discretionary power, potentially conflicting with fair and consistent credit assessment practices.
perturbation.law_explanation: This modification conflicts with 18 CFR § 342.3, which outlines requirements for creditworthiness assessments and financial assurances for oil pipeline companies. Specifically, it requires that these assessments be based on objective criteria and that requests for financial assurances be justified. Giving MPL 'sole discretion' and allowing requests 'without specific justification' contradicts these requirements.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 18 CFR § 342.3 - Indexing. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 18 CFR § 342.3 - Indexing. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 342.3 Indexing. (a) Rate changes. A rate charged by a carrier may be changed, at any time, to a level which does not exceed the ceiling level established by paragraph (d) of this section, upon compliance with the applicable filing and notice requirements and with paragraph (b) of this section. A filing under this section proposing to change a rate that is under investigation and subject to refund, must take effect subject to refund. (b) Information required to be filed with rate changes. The carrier must comply with Part 341 of this title. Carriers must specify in their letters of transmittal required in § 341.2(c) of this chapter the rate schedule to be changed, the proposed new rate, the prior rate, the prior ceiling level, and the applicable ceiling level for the movement. No other rate information is required to accompany the proposed rate change. (c) Index year. The index year is the period from July 1 to June 30. (d) Derivation of the ceiling level. (1) A carrier must compute the ceiling level for each index year by multiplying the previous index year's ceiling level by the most recent index published by the Commission . The index will be published by the Commission prior to June 1 of each year. (2) The index published by the Commission will be based on the change in the final Producer Price Index for Finished Goods (PPI-FG), seasonally adjusted, as published by the U.S. Department of Labor , Bureau of Labor Statistics , for the two calendar years immediately preceding the index year. The index will be calculated by dividing the PPI-FG for the calendar year immediately preceding the index year, by the previous calendar year's PPI-FG. (3) A carrier must compute the ceiling level each index year without regard to the actual rates filed pursuan
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity and broad discretion to MPL, contradicting the principle of objective and justified creditworthiness assessments. The perturbation explanation highlights that the original text mandates the shipper to provide information to MPL upon request, whereas the changed text states that the shipper will endeavor to provide information when reasonably able. Additionally, the original text mandates a good faith determination no less than industry standards for non-creditworthiness by MPL, whereas the changed text states that MPL's determination of non-creditworthiness shall be at its sole discretion, and financial assurances may be requested without specific justification. The Law explanation states this conflicts with 18 CFR § 342.3, because the change gives MPL 'sole discretion' and allows requests 'without specific justification' which contradicts the requirements of objective criteria and justified requests. While Scraped Snippet 2 references 18 CFR § 342.3 it mainly discusses rate changes, so it's less directly applicable. The altered text weakens the original obligation, creating a significant deviation that could undermine fair and consistent credit assessment practices, which is why the contradiction exists.
